summary_one_line: Input must declare a DTD ATTLIST attribute of type ID and instantiate it so an attribute node carries XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID at free time

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed-enough DTD that declares an attribute of type ID (an <!ATTLIST ... ID> / xml:id-style construct) plus an element instance carrying that attribute, so the parser stamps cur->atype = XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID on the attribute node; only then does xmlFreeProp take the true branch at tree.c:2125 calling xmlRemoveID.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach the true branch (T=20) and carry the literal DTD/ID lexical scaffolding: byte-stable DOCTYPE keyword at offsets 0x20-0x27 ('DOCTYPE') and an 'xml:id="' / ':id="' token run, plus 34 hits on the branch; the vpc-only I2SRandReplace mutator splices the logged keyword/attribute-name comparison operands into the input bytes so the parser's string CMPs against 'ID'/the declared attribute name succeed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds stay on the false branch (T=0 F=19, resolved 0/10): their byte content drifts to xlink/SYSTEM/CDATA/IMPLIED scaffolding and never assembles the ATTLIST-ID declaration plus matching attribute instance, so atype never becomes XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID; lacking I2SRandReplace, vp can only push CMP_MAP distance buckets and never lands the exact multi-byte keyword equalities the ID path requires.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace input-to-state mutator that value_profile lacks; I2S copies logged comparison operands (the 'ID' attribute-type keyword and declared attribute names parsed from the DTD) verbatim into input positions, solving the exact-string equality chain that gates atype==XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID. value_profile's distance-bucket feedback alone cannot synthesize the exact byte sequences, so it stalls.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires both cur->doc != NULL and cur->atype == XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID.
Taking the true branch enters the xmlRemoveID body, the ID-removal path.
The winner resolved the branch in all 10 trials while the loser was blocked in all 10.
Winner-resolving seeds carry DTD scaffolding including the DOCTYPE keyword.
A winner seed contains an xml:id attribute token, matching the ID-attribute requirement.
The winner takes the blocker true side (T=20) while the loser never does (T=0 F=19).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=20 F=0  T=0 F=19  if ((cur->doc != NULL) && (cur->atype == XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID...

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog is the only involved fuzzer that has the I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only has havoc plus token mutations.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6936 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed c20581314eb72916` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 of the 7-level ancestor chain, the ParentInfo-only signature of an I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with an ATTLIST-declared ID attribute where value_profile resolves the atype==XML_ATTRIBUTE_ID branch as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the decisive factor.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison operand I2S splices (the literal 'ID' keyword vs the declared attribute name) is inferred from source semantics, not directly observed in the seed-byte diff at a single fixed offset.
